description: Use this API to export harmonics distortion calculation results to comma separated (.csv) file. The harmonic analysis must be previously performed and working memory contains those results. Three CSV files are created with ‘_volt’, ‘_flow’ and ‘_cursrc’ suffixes added to the input file name. File with ‘_volt’ suffix contains bus voltages. File with’_flow’ suffix contains flows in all network elements and equipment. File with ‘_cursrc’ suffix contains harmonic current source spectrum applied in distortion calculations. For harmonic order 1, the column “%THDor%INDV” provides %THD (percent total harmonic distortion) and for all other harmonic orders it provides %INDV (percent individual harmonic in terms of fundamental). This API routine was first introduced in release 35.4.2. ierr = har_export_dstn(csvname, intgar)

parameters.name: csvname
parameters.description: Name of the file to which harmonics distortion calculation results are exported. (input; no default allowed).

parameters.name: intgar
parameters.description: An array of three elements specifying Integer Options for quantities to export. (input).

intgar.description: Bus Voltage Export Option (1 by default), can also be specified with keyword ‘voltoptn’.
Export bus voltages for HORD=1 (only THDs exported).
Export bus voltages for all HORD (THDs and individual harmonic levels exported).
Current Flow Export Option (0 by default), can also be specified with keyword ‘flowoptn’.
Export flows for HORD=1 (only THDs exported).
Export flows for all HORD (THDs and individual harmonic levels exported).
Harmonic Current Source Spectrum Option (0 by default), can also be specified with keyword ‘cursrcoptn’.
Export harmonic current sources for HORD=1 (only THDs exported).
Export harmonic current sources for all HORD (THDs and individual harmonic levels exported).

error_codes.description: prerequisite requirements for API are not met.
error opening all output CSV files.
error no INTGAR option to export specified.
